new_exact: Such preparatory work might include factual investigations and discovery in connection with litigation or an administrative proceeding where the lawyer reasonably expects to be admitted pro hac vice.
source: /tmp/src_2026ND109.txt - PDF page showing Comment [5] quoted in [¶24]
evidence: The PDF source clearly shows the correct phrase sequence: 'Such preparatory work might include factual investigations and discovery in connection with litigation...' The DB text has the words scrambled, appearing to be an OCR/ingestion error where line-wrapping caused words to be reordered: 'Such investigations and preparatory work might discovery...include factual' - the adverb 'might', verb 'discovery' (should be part of 'might include'), and the adjective-noun phrase 'include factual' are all out of sequence. This is a high-confidence fix verified directly against the source PDF.
note: Located in [¶24], which quotes N.D.R. Prof. Conduct 5.5, Comment [5]. The scrambling appears to have occurred at a line/column break in the PDF where multi-line quoted text was extracted.
